decks.notes: This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan and controlling the board through land destruction and removal spells. It makes use of cards like Erode, Path to Exile, Galvanic Discharge, and Wrath of the Skies to keep the opponent's threats in check. The deck also includes Price of Freedom and High Noon to generate advantage and put pressure on the opponent. Additionally, it utilizes cards like Field of Ruin and Cleansing Wildfire to disrupt the opponent's mana base. By combining control elements with land destruction, this deck aims to outvalue and outlast its opponent.

The deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with powerful creatures and then bringing them back onto the battlefield to overwhelm the opponent. It uses cards like Persist, Unearth, and Faithless Looting to put creatures in the graveyard, while also packing disruption in the form of spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. The goal is to reanimate high-impact creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones to quickly close out the game before the opponent can respond effectively. The inclusion of Abhorrent Oculus and Psychic Frog adds additional control elements to the combo strategy.

The main concept of this Modern Aggro-Affinity deck is to quickly assemble a strong board presence by playing cheap artifacts and using cards like Mox Opal and Urza's Saga to generate mana efficiently. The deck then aims to pressure opponents with aggressive creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary while disrupting their game plan with cards like Tormod's Crypt and Metallic Rebuke. Emry, Lurker of the Loch provides card advantage and recursion for artifacts, while cards like Engineered Explosives and Arcbound Ravager offer flexibility in dealing with various threats on the board.

The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy, utilizing low-cost creatures and removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Voice of Victory. Cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls help maintain pressure on the opponent, while Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provides additional support and damage potential. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Blood Moon and Static Prison to disrupt the opponent's strategy and gain an advantage. Overall, the Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly building a strong board presence and controlling the pace of the game.

The deck focuses on quickly establishing a board presence with artifacts, using cards like Mox Opal and Mishra's Bauble to power out threats such as Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary. Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke provide disruption and removal, while Urza's Saga generates additional value. The deck's aggressive strategy is bolstered by cards like Galvanic Blast and Shadowspear, aiming to overwhelm opponents with a combination of artifact creatures and direct damage. Welding Jar and Claws of Gix provide protection and utility, creating a fast-paced, aggressive playstyle centered around the powerful synergy of affinity for artifacts.

The primary focus of this deck is to use the Amulet of Vigor to generate additional mana and untap lands, enabling the player to efficiently ramp into powerful threats such as Primeval Titan and Cultivator Colossus. By utilizing cards like Scapeshift and Green Sun's Zenith, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a strong board presence. Additionally, cards like Urza's Saga, Tolaria West, and Boseiju, Who Endures provide additional utility and consistency to the deck's game plan, allowing for flexibility and adaptability in various matchups.

The deck focuses on using a mix of aggressive creatures and burn spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It also utilizes sacrifice effects and control elements like Static Prison and Blood Moon to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to gain incremental advantages with cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Seasoned Pyromancer, while maintaining a low curve and putting pressure on the opponent with creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Voice of Victory. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah also provides a powerful finisher to close out games quickly.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan by using a variety of control elements such as counter spells, discard spells, removal, and card advantage. It aims to control the game and slow down the opponent's threats while setting up its win conditions through powerful creatures like Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares. The deck also includes card draw and selection tools to maintain consistency and ensure that the player has the right answers at the right time. The mana base is optimized for casting the spells efficiently and consistently.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly building up a board presence with aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, supported by burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Lightning Bolt. The deck also includes utility cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Seasoned Pyromancer for card advantage and flexibility. The inclusion of Goblin Bombardment adds a sacrifice outlet for additional reach and synergizes well with the deck's aggressive strategy. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast and efficient creature-based game plan backed up by versatile removal and disruption spells.

The deck focuses on aggressive creature plays combined with the ability to repeatedly exile and return creatures to generate value and disrupt the opponent's game plan. By utilizing blink effects like Ephemerate and Flickerwisp, the deck can trigger powerful enter-the-battlefield abilities multiple times. Cards like Solitude and Overlord of the Balemurk provide strong threats on the battlefield, while disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Teferi, Time Raveler help control the game. The deck also includes a mix of removal and card draw to maintain tempo and pressure the opponent.

The deck focuses on using instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance to quickly bring back powerful creatures like Griselbrand onto the battlefield. It also includes cards like Faithful Mending and Solitude to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck aims to combine these elements efficiently to create a powerful combo that can overwhelm the opponent and secure victory. By leveraging these key cards and strategies, the deck can consistently execute its game plan and emerge victorious in a 1 vs 1 Modern format.

This deck focuses on maintaining a consistent flow of aggro pressure through a mix of creatures like Gravecrawler and Knight-Errant of Eos, along with removal spells like Fatal Push. It also includes cards that generate value over time, such as Marionette Apprentice and Guide of Souls. The deck aims to control the board while steadily building up a powerful board presence. The mana base is optimized for smooth and efficient casting of spells, ensuring that the deck can operate at its maximum potential from the early game onwards.

This deck focuses on using Amulet of Vigor to speed up the process of ramping into powerful spells like Primeval Titan and Scapeshift. By utilizing cards like Arboreal Grazer and Simic Growth Chamber, the deck is able to quickly generate mana and play multiple spells in a single turn. Additionally, cards like Urza's Saga and Tolaria West provide additional utility and tutoring options. By combining ramp, land synergy, and powerful combo pieces, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with big threats and efficient plays.

This deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Tower, Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant) to generate a large amount of mana quickly. It utilizes powerful control elements such as Chalice of the Void, Trinisphere, and Thought-Knot Seer to disrupt opponents' strategies and protect its own game plan. The deck also includes powerful threats like Ugin, Karn, and Emrakul to close out the game once the Tron lands are assembled. Overall, the deck aims to control the game early on and overwhelm opponents with its powerful late-game threats.

The deck focuses on quickly deploying artifacts onto the battlefield and using them to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive and efficient creatures. It aims to control the board with spells like Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke while also generating additional value through cards like Mishra's Bauble and Urza's Saga. The deck's key strength lies in its ability to quickly build a threatening board presence and disrupt the opponent's game plan, giving it a strong edge in the early to mid-game.

The main concept of the deck revolves around using Eldrazi creatures and ramping up mana to cast powerful spells and abilities that control the game. The deck focuses on playing Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Sire of Seven Deaths, along with spells like Kozilek's Command and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The ramp elements help quickly get to higher mana levels, allowing for the casting of expensive spells like World Breaker and Malevolent Rumble to dominate the game.
